_meta.description: Per-umpire HR and K multipliers applied to batter scoring. 1.0 = neutral. Any umpire NOT listed resolves to _default (1.0), so this is purely additive — populating it only helps.
_meta.hrRange: 0.93-1.07. Effect is intentionally tiny: the multiplier hits only the env component (25% of the score), so the max score swing is ~+/-1.75%.
_meta.kRange: 0.93-1.07. Tight zones (low hrFactor) call more strikes → more Ks. Applied as a direct lambda multiplier in the K Brain.
_meta.method: v1 conservative, cross-year-validated. Per-ump HR/Game (2024) -> hrFactor vs league-average ump, deviation compressed to 30% and clamped to +/-0.07. kFactor derived from inverse zone-size relationship: tight HR zone → more strikeouts. See server/tools/compute_umpire_factors.py.
_meta.todo: Replace kFactor with direct K/game derivation once we have ~3+ months of reconciled data (scoredBatters x pitcherKs x homePlateUmpire join). Keyed by boxscore official fullName.
sourceCandidates: Internal: scoredBatters x homerersByGame x umpire join, once we have history

zoneStyles._doc: Dominant zone tendency keyed by umpire name. 'high' = generous with up-in-zone pitches; 'low' = prefers low-zone calls; 'wide' = expansive overall strike zone. Absent = neutral. Used in K Brain to multiply the base kFactor by a pitch-location x umpire interaction term.
